Art title 1: Image Projection is Image Protection (by Anthony Gurrola)
Artist Statement 1:
Often times the one talking the most about themselves, don't really know who they are.

They are trying to tell you who they are before they are ever truly seen.

To be seen is terrifying.

To be known, fully known, can be the hardest thing for someone.

What if they see all my mess?

What if they see I am imperfect?

What if they see that I actually struggle with something no one expected?

So instead of being known,

We protect our clean image.

The image of who we try to be.

But the reality is,

Who we try to be, and who God has called us to be are two vastly different people.

Different in the sense that who God calls us to be is infinity more beautiful than anything we can imagine.

We don't see ourselves righteous, so we try to get as close as we can.

In doing so, we subtly say "my sin is greater than your goodness"

This image of ourselves is an idol.

No sin, or dirt on earth can stand against the goodness of God.

Our mess melts in the very presence of His goodness.

The image we have projected is burned away

And what is left is a peice of clay waiting to be shaped.

Let Him tell you who you are,

I promise you it is more extravagant than anything you could have imagined.

The same God that made the sun, the moon, the sea, and the stars handmade you.

As beautiful, and glorious as His creation is,

You are the one He died for.

He is calling you by name.

Learn that name.

(Anthony Gurrola)
Story Title again:
BLOG:
Why God Doesn't Speak During Loss
excerpt:
I've realized mourning is a process you can't rush.

And I think maybe this is why for most of the time in it, it seems like God doesn't really say anything. You know, I guess sometimes I hear things like, "I think you're beautiful." Or "wait" but there's no solution to the pain. Nothing we can do about it. He's just with me. I know I usually try to fix things. But in the situation of grief, we just cry in his presence, and wait. 

It feels like when my mom used to hold me and rock me in that old creaky rocking chair, waiting for the crying to run its course. Nothing to fix. The sadness just has to run its course.

And I think that's the point. She wanted the pain to end for me if she can but in some cases the point is just to be with me while I pour out my pain.

And I think that's maybe why God seems kind of silent during my grief.

Raw Spoon, 

8-24, 2020
3dNotes1: Paran wilderness: After Hagar and Ishmael fled, he lived in Paran. This is the modern day bordre between Israel and Jordan. The dead sea is North from here. You can sea the dead sea through the haze.
3dNotes2: Tel Be'er Sheba: a proposed area where Isaac is said to have been born. Also in this region is possibly where Hagar and Ishmael were provided for in the wilderness.
